paper_title: Sedimentary facies, depositional environments and conceptual outcrop analogue (Dam Formation, early Miocene) Eastern Arabian Platform, Saudi Arabia: a new high-resolution approach

methods.description: This method involves selecting and analyzing outcrops to understand sedimentological features, facies, and stratigraphic patterns in the Dam Formation.

steps.description: About five outcrops were selected along the NW-SE transect, from forty outcrops in the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: select outcrops for study along NW-SE transect

steps.description: The outcrops were sedimentologically logged in terms of facies, cyclicities, and stratigraphic stacking patterns.
Sampling was done with an overall space of 30 cm, mostly bed by bed.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from outcrops at 30 cm intervals

steps.description: 110 thin sections and 100 polished slabs were examined for sedimentological analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: examine thin sections and slabs for sedimentological analysis

methods.description: This method uses high-resolution photography and digital tools to create a detailed analogue model of the outcrops.

steps.description: About 800 photographs were taken from each outcrop using a Canon EOS 4000D Kit EF-S 18-55 III.
All photographs were geo-referenced using Picasa version 3.9.
The interpreted photographs were imported into CorelDRAW, overlapped, and field descriptions were digitized.
steps.searchable_summary: import and digitize photographs in CorelDRAW

steps.description: The stratigraphic surfaces and facies boundaries were marked in the overlapped final image.
steps.searchable_summary: mark stratigraphic surfaces and facies boundaries

methods.description: This method involves laboratory techniques to analyze mineralogical composition and textural features of samples.

steps.description: Samples were processed using standard thin section preparation, XRD, and SEM-EDS techniques.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples using thin section, XRD, and SEM-EDS

steps.description: Thin sections were studied using an Olympus optical petrographic microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze thin sections with petrographic microscope

steps.description: Photomicrographs from both thin section and SEM images were digitized and described using JMicroVision software.
steps.searchable_summary: digitize and describe photomicrographs with JMicroVision
